name: Bread Soup
recipeIngredient: 2 ripe plaintains or bananas peeled, sliced into 1/4 inch pieces
1 bunch oregano or 1/2 tablespoon dried oregano
1/2 bunch thyme, or 1/2 tablespoon dried thyme
sprig rosemary or 1/4 teaspoon dried rosemary
1 1/2 cups canned tomatoes, diced with juice
1/3 loaf baguette, cut into 1/2 inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium-low heat.
2. Cook the onions with the salt until they start to caramelize, about 10 to 15 minutes.
3. Add the plantains or bananas and cook for an additional 10 minutes.
4. Stir in the garlic, oregano, thyme, and rosemary. Cook just until aromatic, about 3 minutes longer.
5. Add the tomatoes, bread chunks, and chicken or vegetable stock. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer.
6. Cook, stirring frequently, until the bread has softened and the flavors are well blended, about 45 minutes.
7. Ladle the soup into bowls.
8. Squeeze a wedge of lime into each bowl and toss in another wedge for garnish.
9. Serve hot.
